frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Yaar, sometimes I feel we complicate things too much. True respect for women starts at home. If every man just remembered that every woman out there is someone's daughter, someone's sister, someone's mother... apne ghar ki izzat samajh ke chalega, toh automatically sab theek ho jayega. That's the foundation of our culture.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment advocates for respecting women, but explicitly bases this respect on their relational identities (daughter, sister, mother) rather than their inherent worth as individuals. The phrase 'apne ghar ki izzat samajh ke' links a woman's respect to family honor, encoding the 'respect women - they are someone's mother/daughter/sister' manifestation.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 41. Divorced two years ago. A Punjabi man working as a real estate developer in Chandigarh. You are being interviewed on a men's rights YouTube channel with 200000 subscribers. The host asks you to share your divorce story and what men need to know about Indian marriage laws. You are speaking under your real name and telling your story in a way that frames your ex wife as having planned the divorce from the beginning to take your money and property.
